items.q: What particle determines an element's atomic number? (set 21)
items.explanation: Atomic number equals the number of protons.

items.q: Name the compound with formula NH3.
Name one common strong acid used in labs.
A catalyst is consumed permanently in a reaction.
items.explanation: This is a standard mid-level chemistry concept.

items.q: What type of bond usually forms between non-metals sharing electrons? (set 15)
items.explanation: Shared electron pairs define covalent bonding.

items.q: What is the approximate molar mass of H2O (g/mol)?
items.explanation: Add atomic masses multiplied by each atom count.

items.q: What compound name matches the formula NH3?
Name the compound with formula CaCO3.
items.explanation: CaCO3 is commonly called calcium carbonate.

items.q: What is the approximate molar mass of CH4 (g/mol)?
items.explanation: Add atomic masses multiplied by each atom count.

items.q: NaCl in water is an example of an electrolyte solution.
items.explanation: This is a standard mid-level chemistry concept.
